svg.title: Unification of cons()≠()! with Q(S) and factorial lattice
svg.desc: Structural diagram showing how the binary quadratic exclusion invariant maps onto the 4!/5!/6!/7! sexagesimal lattice and the cons/()! duality.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the cons() null manifold in the binary quadratic lexer')
sendPrompt('Explain the ()! terminal fixed point in the binary quadratic lexer')

svg.text: the quadratic form proves which side of the boundary you are on

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does it mean for an instruction to lie on the zero-surface Q(S)=0?')
g.text: every LL agrees · all delimiters exact
N, M pass freely as payload rays

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Q(S)=1 mean geometrically in the quadratic lexer?')

svg.text: squares prevent error cancellation → Q=0 iff all constraints =0
the 128-bit word is the geometric medium — 8 × 16-bit segments = 8 vertices of a projective cube
factorial layers provide the dimensional scaffolding around the 4! Omicron frame

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the 4! Omicron projective encapsulation in the sexagesimal lattice?')
sendPrompt('What role does 5!=120 play as the sexagesimal stride?')
g.text: coordinate lattice N and M traverse once Q(S)=0

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the 6!=720 promote boundary do?')
g.text: promote boundary · GC fires · carry propagates

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the 7!=5040 master replay ring?')
g.text: full state space · tick mod 5040 · SharedArrayBuffer

svg.text: at every layer: valid state = zero-surface (cons())
terminal state = first error layer (()!) · 0 ≠ 1 holds everywhere
